title: CNN This Morning · 2026-06-15 · Kesepakatan AS-Iran tentang Selat Hormuz
summary: Amerika Serikat dan Iran mencapai kesepakatan untuk membuka Selat Hormuz pada hari Jumat, tetapi Iran menuntut AS mencabut blokade, menghentikan aksi militer, dan mencairkan dana, sementara AS membantah akan segera mentransfer dana. Mantan Presiden Obama meragukan efektivitas kesepakatan tersebut. Israel tidak puas karena kesepakatan tidak mencakup masalah nuklir dan kelompok proksi. Selain itu, sebuah pesawat terjun payung jatuh dan semua penumpang tewas, Senator McConnell dirawat di rumah sakit, Inggris berencana memberlakukan larangan media sosial, UFC mengadakan pertandingan di halaman Gedung Putih, dan Carolina Hurricanes memenangkan Piala Stanley.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Today in the group chat, both the U.S. and Iran say there's an agreement to open the Strait of Hormuz on Friday. Why not open it right away?
paragraphs.cn: Hari ini dalam obrolan grup, AS dan Iran sama-sama mengatakan telah mencapai kesepakatan untuk membuka Selat Hormuz pada hari Jumat. Mengapa tidak segera dibuka?

paragraphs.speaker: Mike Waltz
paragraphs.en: The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators.
paragraphs.cn: Orang Iran adalah lawan negosiasi yang sangat sulit.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And former President Obama says he's doubtful Trump's agreement will be much different than his. True or just sour grapes?
paragraphs.cn: Mantan Presiden Obama mengatakan dia ragu kesepakatan Trump akan jauh berbeda dengan kesepakatannya. Apakah ini fakta atau rasa iri?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Just getting to enjoy God's green Earth and then have something like this turn so tragic.
paragraphs.cn: Baru saja menikmati bumi hijau ciptaan Tuhan, lalu terjadi hal seperti ini, menjadi sangat tragis.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: A plane full of skydivers crashes right after takeoff, killing everyone on board. We're going to talk about what happened moments before it went down. And Senator Mitch McConnell hospitalized. What we know about his condition this morning.
paragraphs.cn: Sebuah pesawat yang membawa penerjun payung jatuh tak lama setelah lepas landas, semua penumpang tewas. Kami akan membahas apa yang terjadi sebelum pesawat jatuh. Pemimpin Minoritas Senat Mitch McConnell dirawat di rumah sakit. Kami mengetahui kondisinya pagi ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Too much time spent on screens is not good for anyone. Never mind someone's brain is still developing.
paragraphs.cn: Menatap layar terlalu lama tidak baik bagi siapa pun. Apalagi bagi mereka yang otaknya masih berkembang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: In the U.K., they want to give their kids their childhood back. But will a new social media ban actually work?
paragraphs.cn: Di Inggris, mereka ingin mengembalikan masa kecil kepada anak-anak. Tetapi apakah larangan media sosial yang baru benar-benar akan berhasil?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Our country was built on fighting against -- you know, for independence. UFC fight night at the White House lawn. What could the event's legacy be? And the Carolina Hurricanes are Stanley Cup champions, capping off the season with a shutout over the Las Vegas Golden Knights. A diplomatic breakthrough: The U.S. and Iran say they have reached a deal to end the war and to reopen the strait of Hormuz. It will take effect on Friday. We're going to talk about why they're waiting. J.D. VANCE, U.S.
paragraphs.cn: Negara kita didirikan atas perlawanan -- -- Anda tahu, memperjuangkan kemerdekaan. Malam pertarungan UFC di halaman Gedung Putih. Warisan apa yang akan ditinggalkan acara ini? Carolina Hurricanes menutup musim dengan menaklukkan Vegas Golden Knights dan memenangkan Piala Stanley. Sebuah terobosan diplomatik: AS dan Iran mengatakan telah mencapai kesepakatan untuk mengakhiri perang dan membuka kembali Selat Hormuz. Kesepakatan itu akan berlaku pada hari Jumat. Kami akan membahas mengapa mereka menunggu. Wakil Presiden AS J.D. Vance (sambungan telepon):

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: VICE PRESIDENT (via phone): Sometimes these ceasefires take a little bit of time to take root. People are very good over there at fighting each other. I'm not going to say that everybody is going to sing kumbaya tomorrow. It's going to take a little bit of time to learn the ways of peace. But I do think we took a major, major step.
paragraphs.cn: Wakil Presiden (sambungan telepon): Terkadang gencatan senjata seperti ini membutuhkan waktu untuk mengakar. Orang-orang di sana sangat pandai saling bertarung. Saya tidak akan mengatakan besok semua orang akan bernyanyi 'Kumbaya' bersama. Belajar cara berdamai membutuhkan waktu. Tapi saya yakin kita telah mengambil langkah yang sangat signifikan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to begin with the commitment from the U.S. and Iran to end the months- long conflict. But already, there's a bit of a snag. Tehran saying that 60 days of nuclear negotiations will begin once the U.S. meets three commitments. They want the U.S. to end the naval blockade; end military operations against Iran; and release billions of dollars in frozen Iranian funds.
paragraphs.cn: Selamat pagi semuanya. Saya Ode Cornish. Kita mulai dengan AS dan Iran yang berjanji untuk mengakhiri konflik berbulan-bulan. Tapi sudah ada sedikit masalah. Teheran mengatakan bahwa perundingan nuklir selama 60 hari akan dimulai setelah AS memenuhi tiga janji. Mereka menuntut AS mengakhiri blokade laut; menghentikan aksi militer terhadap Iran; dan mencairkan dana Iran senilai miliaran dolar.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: KAZEM GHARIBABADI, IRAN DEPUTY FOREIGN MINISTER (through translator): This memorandum of understanding was reached, despite our complete distrust of our enemies. We have no trust in the enemy. Our trust lies in our military strength, our diplomatic capabilities, and the support and unity of our own people. That is why the implementation of this memorandum of understanding is extremely important. We have specific plans in place to monitor the implementation of U.S. commitments under this memorandum.
paragraphs.cn: Wakil Menteri Luar Negeri Iran Kazem Gharibabadi (melalui penerjemah): Meskipun kami sama sekali tidak percaya pada musuh, nota kesepahaman ini tercapai. Kami tidak percaya pada musuh. Yang kami percaya adalah kekuatan militer, kemampuan diplomatik, serta dukungan dan persatuan rakyat kami. Itulah mengapa sangat penting untuk melaksanakan nota kesepahaman ini. Kami memiliki rencana konkret untuk mengawasi pemenuhan komitmen AS berdasarkan nota ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But a U.S. official is saying that no funds will be released before Iran implements its commitments. So, could this derail things? I'm going to bring in CNN global affairs commentator and former deputy Pentagon press secretary, Sabrina Singh. So, Sabrina, good morning. Thank you for being here.
paragraphs.cn: Tetapi seorang pejabat AS mengatakan tidak ada dana yang akan dicairkan sampai Iran memenuhi komitmennya. Lalu, apakah ini akan menggagalkan perkembangan? Mari kita undang Sabrina Singh, komentator urusan global CNN dan mantan wakil sekretaris pers Pentagon. Sabrina, selamat pagi. Terima kasih sudah datang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, first, I want to just talk about the latest Iran saying, Look, we're not doing anything until money is unfrozen. And then the U.S. disputing that. Here's the U.S. ambassador to the U.N., Mike Waltz, speaking on Sunday. We'll talk on the other side of that.
paragraphs.cn: Pertama, saya ingin membahas pernyataan terbaru Iran: mereka mengatakan kami tidak akan melakukan apa pun sampai dana dicairkan. Dan AS membantah hal ini. Ini adalah pernyataan Duta Besar AS untuk PBB Mike Waltz pada hari Minggu. Kita akan bahas nanti.

paragraphs.speaker: Waltz
paragraphs.en: The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators, coupled -- coupled with the fact that they're having a very hard time getting guidance from their supreme leader. And they're not always on the same page within their team, between the civilians and military. So, you know, the team has had their hands full dealing with them. But the president has every intent to get this done.
paragraphs.cn: Orang Iran adalah lawan negosiasi yang sangat sulit, ditambah lagi mereka sulit mendapatkan instruksi dari pemimpin tertinggi. Dan di dalam tim mereka, pendapat antara sipil dan militer tidak selalu selaras. Jadi, Anda tahu, tim AS sudah sangat sibuk berurusan dengan mereka. Tapi presiden benar-benar berniat menyelesaikan ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He sort of nodded to something, I think, that maybe people don't fully appreciate, which is that Iran's regime is pretty much in place, even as though a tier of leadership was killed in these strikes. So, answer the question that he's raising here of, like, negotiation, the money. What are the complications?
paragraphs.cn: Dia sepertinya mengisyaratkan sesuatu, saya pikir mungkin orang tidak sepenuhnya menyadari, bahwa rezim Iran pada dasarnya masih stabil, meskipun satu lapisan kepemimpinan telah dilenyapkan dalam serangan-serangan ini. Lalu, menjawab pertanyaannya: negosiasi, dana. Di mana letak kerumitannya?

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: The complications here is that Iran is very skilled at negotiating. And this regime, in particular, is more hardline than the previous regime. I mean, they are willing to incur the costs and the military strikes that the U.S. and Israel have conducted over, we've seen, a significant amount of time. I think what's interesting about this memorandum of understanding, or this new agreement, is it's only there to open the straits.
paragraphs.cn: Kerumitannya adalah Iran sangat pandai bernegosiasi. Dan rezim ini khususnya lebih keras dari rezim sebelumnya. Maksud saya, mereka bersedia menanggung biaya serangan militer yang berkepanjangan dari AS dan Israel. Saya pikir yang menarik dari nota kesepahaman atau kesepakatan baru ini adalah bahwa itu hanya untuk membuka selat.

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: It does not address the thorniest issues, which we know Iran is really going to drag its feet on, which are the nuclear -- its nuclear program and the highly enriched uranium that is still sitting in Iran. And so, while this agreement basically tries to restore the status to pre-war status, it still doesn't address those nuclear agreements. And Iran, like -- like Mike Waltz was saying, Iran knows how to play the game to draw the United States out.
paragraphs.cn: Ini tidak menyelesaikan masalah paling rumit, yang kita tahu akan ditunda-tunda Iran, yaitu program nuklirnya dan uranium yang diperkaya tinggi yang masih disimpan di Iran. Jadi, meskipun kesepakatan ini berusaha mengembalikan situasi ke status sebelum perang, tetap saja tidak menyelesaikan perjanjian nuklir tersebut. Dan Iran, seperti yang dikatakan Mike Waltz, tahu cara bermain untuk mengulur waktu AS.

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: If we're talking about negotiating, at minimum, within 60 days, that is bringing us up right up to the midterms. And I think Iran knows that the United States is not going to want to start up kinetic action right before the midterms, right before voting starts in (UNINTELLIGIBLE) --
paragraphs.cn: Jika kita bicara tentang negosiasi, setidaknya 60 hari, itu tepat bertepatan dengan pemilihan paruh waktu. Saya pikir Iran tahu AS tidak akan ingin memulai aksi militer sebelum pemilihan paruh waktu, sebelum pemungutan suara dimulai.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Two military questions. One, when we say the strait is opening, does that mean that they're going to -- Iran just backs down, the U.S. backs down? Are there mines in the strait? Like, are there still dangers there?
paragraphs.cn: Dua masalah militer. Pertama, ketika kita mengatakan selat terbuka, apakah itu berarti Iran mengalah, AS mengalah? Apakah ada ranjau di selat? Apakah masih ada bahaya di sana?

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: I think both sides could say today that the strait is open and that they're clearing the mines from the strait. But the only ones that are going to determine if the strait is open are the shipping companies. Are they willing to allow their ships to start going through the strait and potentially incurring costs if, let's say, a drone is launched from Iran or there's crossfire between the two sides? So, even though Donald Trump is saying on Friday the strait is open, it is still --
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir kedua belah pihak hari ini bisa mengatakan selat terbuka dan ranjau sedang dibersihkan. Tapi satu-satunya yang bisa menentukan apakah selat terbuka adalah perusahaan pelayaran. Apakah mereka bersedia mengirim kapal mulai melewati selat, dan mungkin menanggung biaya, seperti drone Iran atau tembakan dari kedua belah pihak? Jadi, meskipun Donald Trump pada hari Jumat mengatakan selat terbuka, itu masih -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We won't know until ships pass through.
paragraphs.cn: Kita harus menunggu kapal melewati untuk mengetahuinya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Last thing militarily, Israel.
paragraphs.cn: Terakhir, aspek militer, Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Israel, we're going to talk about in this program, across the political spectrum, unhappy with this deal. And in part because of military reasons. They say it doesn't address their issues around proxies that Iran backs like Hezbollah. It doesn't even talk about ballistic missiles. That's not even in the conversation around the memorandum of understanding, at least so far. This is kind of the same criticism that the Obama deal had.
paragraphs.cn: Israel, yang akan kita bahas di segmen ini, semua kalangan politik tidak puas dengan kesepakatan ini. Sebagian karena pertimbangan militer. Mereka mengatakan kesepakatan ini tidak menyelesaikan masalah proksi yang didukung Iran (seperti Hizbullah). Bahkan tidak menyebut rudal balistik. Setidaknya untuk saat ini, hal itu bahkan tidak disinggung dalam pembahasan nota kesepahaman. Ini mirip dengan kritik yang diterima kesepakatan Obama.

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: I mean, you might not have agreed with the JCPOA, but that was something -- under the Obama administration. But that was something that took almost two years to negotiate. Now, with Israel, what's interesting about this agreement that this administration has negotiated, Israel is not party to the agreement. And they have said -- I mean, I think as early as this morning, that they might continue to conduct operations in Lebanon. So, between now and Friday, things could get derailed very quickly.
paragraphs.cn: Maksud saya, Anda mungkin tidak setuju dengan Rencana Aksi Komprehensif Bersama (JCPOA), tetapi itu adalah kesepakatan yang dicapai pada masa pemerintahan Obama, yang memakan waktu hampir dua tahun untuk dinegosiasikan. Dan sekarang, mengenai kesepakatan yang dinegosiasikan oleh pemerintahan ini, yang menarik adalah Israel bukan pihak dalam kesepakatan itu. Mereka mengatakan -- saya pikir paling awal pagi ini -- bahwa mereka mungkin akan melanjutkan operasi di Lebanon. Jadi, dari sekarang hingga Jumat, situasinya bisa cepat lepas kendali.

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: I think President Trump, he wanted a win on his birthday, which he -- which he got this agreement. And he also wanted to walk into the G-7 with saying, Hey, this war is coming to an end. European partners, now it's time to do your part and help us carry on the strait (ph).
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir Presiden Trump ingin meraih kemenangan di hari ulang tahunnya, dan dia memang mendapatkan kesepakatan ini. Dia juga ingin memasuki KTT G7 dengan sikap 'Hei, perang ini akan segera berakhir'. Mitra-mitra Eropa, sekarang saatnya Anda melakukan bagian Anda untuk membantu kami terus menjaga situasi di Selat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can we do that if Israel has seized an amount of land that's, like, around the size of New York City.
paragraphs.cn: Jika Israel menduduki wilayah sebesar Kota New York, bisakah kita tetap melakukannya?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And says it's going to continue its -- its battle against Hezbollah?
paragraphs.cn: Dan mengatakan akan terus menyerang Hizbullah?

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: I think it's going to be very difficult if Israel continues to conduct military operations for -- for Iran to agree to terms with the United States when it's going to say to the United States, this is also an aggressor in this war that also struck our country. You need to pull them back. And that's why you saw the president sort of react in some of these interviews that he was giving on Sunday, saying, you know, what is Bibi doing? Because he does not want to see this agreement go off the rails.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir, jika Israel melanjutkan operasi militer, akan sulit bagi Iran untuk mencapai kesepakatan dengan AS, karena Iran akan berkata kepada AS, 'Anda juga agresor dalam perang ini, dan Anda juga menyerang negara kami. Anda harus membuat mereka mundur.' Itulah mengapa Anda melihat presiden bereaksi dalam beberapa wawancara pada hari Minggu, mengatakan, 'Anda tahu, apa yang dilakukan Bibi?' Karena dia tidak ingin melihat kesepakatan ini tergelincir.

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: OK, Sabrina, thank you so much for giving us some of the update there. Coming up on CNN THIS MORNING, President Trump, as Sabrina just said, has been calling out Benjamin Netanyahu. So, we're going to talk about why Trump would be saying that Netanyahu has, quote, "no 'F'-ing judgment' after the Israeli P.M. hit Lebanon right after the announcement of an agreement with Iran. Plus, one of the deadliest U.S. skydiving plane crashes in decades.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, Sabrina, terima kasih banyak telah memberi kami informasi terbaru. Selanjutnya di acara CNN This Morning, seperti yang dikatakan Sabrina, Presiden Trump telah menyebut nama Benjamin Netanyahu. Jadi, kami akan membahas mengapa Trump mengatakan Netanyahu 'tidak memiliki penilaian', karena Perdana Menteri Israel menyerang Lebanon segera setelah pengumuman kesepakatan dengan Iran. Selain itu, salah satu kecelakaan pesawat terjun payung paling mematikan di AS dalam beberapa dekade.

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: We're going to talk about what one witness had to say right before the plane went down. And will you see ICE at the polling stations for the midterms? The homeland security secretary is not ruling it out.
paragraphs.cn: Kami akan membahas apa yang dikatakan seorang saksi mata sebelum pesawat jatuh. Juga, apakah Anda akan melihat petugas ICE di tempat pemungutan suara selama pemilu paruh waktu? Menteri Keamanan Dalam Negeri tidak mengesampingkan kemungkinan itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Markwayne Mullins
paragraphs.en: Why would ICE be there? Because only people who should be voting there is American citizens. There shouldn't be any immigration enforcement.
paragraphs.cn: Mengapa ICE ada di sana? Karena hanya mereka yang seharusnya memilih di sana adalah warga negara AS. Seharusnya tidak ada penegakan imigrasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It was important for President Trump to have a deal with Iran before traveling to Europe for a summit with G-7 leaders. That's what sources are telling CNN. The timing is also crucial, because it came on Trump's 80th birthday and his White House UFC fight to celebrate America's 250th anniversary.
paragraphs.cn: Sangat penting bagi Presiden Trump untuk mencapai kesepakatan dengan Iran sebelum berangkat ke Eropa untuk KTT G7. Ini adalah yang dikatakan sumber kepada CNN. Waktunya juga krusial, karena bertepatan dengan ulang tahun Trump yang ke-80 dan pertandingan UFC di Gedung Putih yang ia selenggarakan untuk merayakan 250 tahun AS.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, we're bringing in the group chat to talk more: Sara Fischer, CNN senior media analyst and senior media reporter at Axios; Elena Schneider, national political reporter, "NOTUS"; and Francesca Chambers, White House correspondent at "USA Today." So, to set this up, I want to play the message the administration was sending, not to Iran, not to Israel, but to the American people. Here is J.D. Vance, talking on "FOX News Sunday" in the aftermath of the announcement of this memorandum.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, kami mengundang grup diskusi untuk membahas lebih lanjut: Analis Media Senior CNN dan Jurnalis Media Senior Axios Sara Fischer; Koresponden Politik Nasional NOTUS Elena Schneider; dan Koresponden Gedung Putih USA Today Francesca Chambers. Sebagai pengantar, saya ingin memutar pesan dari pemerintah, bukan kepada Iran, bukan kepada Israel, tetapi kepada rakyat Amerika. Berikut adalah pernyataan J.D. Vance di Fox News Sunday setelah mengumumkan nota kesepahaman ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: VANCE (via phone): My primary message to the American people is, thank you. Because of your patience, I think that we've solved the problem that has plagued this country again for -- for well before I was even born. But this is just a great thing for the American people. I know that they suffered from high gas prices. The president has certainly been very concerned about that fact. But what we're going to be able to do is drive down the cost of energy, not just now, but for the long term.
paragraphs.cn: Vance (melalui telepon): Pesan utama saya kepada rakyat Amerika adalah, terima kasih. Karena kesabaran Anda, saya pikir kami telah menyelesaikan masalah yang telah mengganggu negara ini bahkan sebelum saya lahir. Tapi ini adalah hal yang sangat baik bagi rakyat Amerika. Saya tahu mereka menanggung harga minyak yang tinggi. Presiden tentu sangat peduli dengan fakta itu. Tapi kami akan mampu menurunkan biaya energi, tidak hanya sekarang, tetapi dalam jangka panjang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Francesca, this seems like the -- specifically, this kind of sentence is the message they had hoped to deliver on this day, of so many things, when the president wanted good news. FRANCESCA CHAMBERS, WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENT, "USA TODAY": Well, and there's still a long way to go until we see a final deal. They want that to come in the next 60 days.
paragraphs.cn: Francesca, ini tampaknya -- secara khusus, kalimat semacam ini adalah pesan yang ingin mereka sampaikan pada hari ini, di tengah banyak hal yang ingin didengar presiden sebagai kabar baik. Koresponden Gedung Putih USA Today Francesca Chambers: Yah, masih panjang jalan sebelum kita melihat kesepakatan final. Mereka berharap dapat mencapainya dalam 60 hari ke depan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But the experts, critics, even supporters of the administration who have come out, you know, in the last 24 hours are saying that the devil is, essentially, in the details. I mean, Iran may have agreed to give the United States the enriched uranium or down blend it. We still don't know what that looks like. We still don't know whether or not they're going to dismantle their nuclear program. The United States says that they will. But again, that all has to be worked out as part of this final agreement.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi para ahli, kritikus, dan bahkan pendukung pemerintah dalam 24 jam terakhir mengatakan, detail adalah kuncinya. Maksud saya, Iran mungkin telah setuju untuk memberikan uranium yang diperkaya kepada AS atau mengencerkannya. Kami masih belum tahu detailnya. Kami masih belum tahu apakah mereka akan membongkar program nuklirnya. AS mengatakan mereka akan melakukannya. Tapi sekali lagi, semua ini harus diselesaikan dalam kesepakatan final.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: This is just a memorandum of understanding.
paragraphs.cn: Ini hanya nota kesepahaman.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And yet, Vance comes out and is, like, thanks for being patient. It's been a tough couple of weeks.
paragraphs.cn: Namun, Vance keluar dan berkata, 'Terima kasih atas kesabaran Anda.' Ini benar-benar beberapa minggu yang sulit.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let's wrap it up. You can feel the hands going like this. (BRUSHES PALMS TOGETHER) Given all the question marks Francesca just raised, what's the danger of telling everyone this? ELENA SCHNEIDER, NATIONAL POLITICAL REPORTER, "NOTUS": Well, look, I think that there is a political reality that they are reacting to, which is the pain that voters are feeling every single day when they go fill up their cars, and are looking at high gas prices, and connect that to the ongoing operations and, you know, what's -- what's
paragraphs.cn: Mari kita simpulkan. Anda bisa merasakan tangan bergerak seperti ini. (Menggosok tangan) Mengingat semua tanda tanya yang baru saja diajukan Francesca, apa bahayanya mengatakan hal ini kepada semua orang? Koresponden Politik Nasional NOTUS Elena Schneider: Yah, saya pikir mereka sedang menghadapi realitas politik, yaitu rasa sakit yang dirasakan pemilih setiap hari saat mengisi bensin, melihat harga minyak yang tinggi, dan menghubungkannya dengan operasi yang sedang berlangsung dan apa yang terjadi di Iran. Mereka ingin merayakan kemenangan secepat mungkin, menunjukkan, 'Baiklah, ini akan berubah. Kita bisa melihat ini akan berubah.' Tantangan yang mereka hadapi adalah, apakah itu benar-benar akan berubah? Karena bahkan jika mereka bisa mencapai semacam kesepakatan di sini; gencatan senjata dipertahankan; mereka mulai bernegosiasi, mendapatkan rantai pasokan ini, membiarkan minyak melewati Selat Hormuz, semua ini tidak akan terjadi segera.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: been happening in Iran. And they want to take a victory lap as soon as possible to point out, say, OK, this is now going to change. We can -- we can see that this is going to change. The challenge for them is, is it actually going to change? Because even if they are able to work out some sort of deal here; the ceasefire holds; they start negotiating on this, getting those supply chains, getting that gas to, you know, through the Strait of Hormuz is not going to happen right away.
paragraphs.cn: Yang terjadi di Iran. Mereka ingin merayakan kemenangan secepat mungkin, menunjukkan, 'Baiklah, ini akan berubah. Kita bisa melihat ini akan berubah.' Tantangan yang mereka hadapi adalah, apakah itu benar-benar akan berubah? Karena bahkan jika mereka bisa mencapai semacam kesepakatan di sini; gencatan senjata dipertahankan; mereka mulai bernegosiasi, mendapatkan rantai pasokan ini, membiarkan minyak melewati Selat Hormuz, semua ini tidak akan terjadi segera.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And experts have said time and again that -- that -- that gas prices are going to take a little while to come back.
paragraphs.cn: Para ahli telah berulang kali mengatakan bahwa harga bensin perlu waktu untuk turun.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. People are looking right now, as you're speaking, at the journey that gas prices will have to take from that peak.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Saat Anda berbicara, orang-orang sedang memperhatikan perjalanan harga bensin dari puncaknya.

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: And it's a long climb back down that peak.
paragraphs.cn: Penurunan dari puncak itu adalah proses yang panjang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And it's -- it's a long climb. The thing is, also, once you say something like that, you kind of start a clock. Like, oh, OK, things are going to get better? When? And when you're looking at disapproval ratings that have moved significantly, when you're looking at questions of specifically how have you handled the economy, for example. And seeing independents, 82 percent disapprove. It feels as though it's a bit of a risk to keep saying no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, it's happening, it's happening.
paragraphs.cn: Dan itu adalah proses yang panjang. Masalahnya, begitu Anda mengatakan hal seperti itu, Anda memulai hitungan mundur. Seperti, 'Oh, baiklah, keadaan akan membaik? Kapan?' Ketika Anda melihat tingkat ketidaksetujuan yang tinggi, ketika Anda melihat masalah spesifik seperti bagaimana Anda menangani ekonomi, melihat 82% pemilih independen mengatakan tidak setuju. Terus-menerus mengatakan 'Tidak, tidak, tidak, tidak, tidak, tidak, tidak, tidak, tidak, keadaan membaik, membaik' tampaknya agak berisiko.

paragraphs.speaker: Sara Fischer
paragraphs.en: Yes. You have a five-month time clock until the midterms. So, that's the horizon that they're working with. And I think most experts will argue that the supply chain issues are interesting, because there's a lag time between, obviously, when ships can get there or not.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Masih lima bulan lagi menuju pemilu paruh waktu. Jadi, itu adalah jangka waktu yang perlu mereka tangani. Saya pikir sebagian besar ahli akan menganggap masalah rantai pasokan menarik, karena jelas, ada waktu tunda apakah kapal bisa sampai di sana.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But can you speak it into existence? Like, if you just say it's -- it's getting better, will it get better?
paragraphs.cn: Tapi bisakah Anda membuatnya menjadi kenyataan dengan mengatakannya? Misalnya, jika Anda hanya mengatakan keadaan membaik, apakah itu akan membaik?

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: You cannot. You cannot. But so, let's say after the 60 days the strait does open. Then you have three months. And that should be enough time, actually, for us to start seeing an impact on oil. And we did see overnight that some of the crude oil prices have gone down on the index. But if there is any delay at that, there's not a lot of wiggle room. And so that's where they run into some problems.
paragraphs.cn: Anda tidak bisa. Anda tidak bisa. Tapi, anggaplah 60 hari kemudian Selat benar-benar terbuka. Maka Anda masih punya tiga bulan. Sebenarnya, itu seharusnya cukup untuk mulai melihat dampaknya pada minyak. Kami memang melihat beberapa indeks harga minyak mentah turun semalam. Tapi jika ada penundaan, tidak banyak ruang gerak. Di situlah mereka akan menghadapi beberapa masalah.

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: If oil prices are still, in some places, above five or anywhere in the top, you know, top half of the $4 range, you're going to have a lot of problems leading up to the midterms.
paragraphs.cn: If oil prices are still above $5 in some places, or in the upper half of the $4 range, you'll have a lot of problems before the midterm elections.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Trump has been so bummed about the way Europe has kind of stayed out of this whole thing. He's going to be meeting with G-7 leaders when they made their various announcements today. I could read them all, but they basically all say they welcome the agreement over there. It seems like they agreed on what they would be saying.
paragraphs.cn: Trump is very frustrated that Europe is basically staying out of it. He will meet with G7 leaders today as they issue various statements. I can read them all, but they basically welcome the deal over there. It looks like they've agreed on what they're going to say.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, Francesca, just heading into today, what is the goal for Trump, who's been kind of dismissive of this group of people? Is he going to say to them, you guys weren't helpful, but it would be helpful if you'd get some ships through.
paragraphs.cn: So, Francesca, what was Trump's goal just before today? He's been somewhat dismissive of this group. Will he tell them, you haven't helped, but if you can get some ships through, would that help?

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.en: I mean, they welcome their help with demining efforts that would help get the Strait of Hormuz fully reopen. But I did want to just touch on one other thing that you were saying about the vice president for a minute, because he was called by Lindsey Graham, the architect of this agreement, and that was really --
paragraphs.cn: I mean, they welcome help with mine-clearing operations, which would help fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz. But I want to touch on something else you said about the Vice President, because Lindsey Graham -- the architect of this deal -- called him, and that really --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He must have been surprised to hear.
paragraphs.cn: He must have been surprised to hear that.

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.en: Well, that was -- but it was a very interesting comment, because he was really put -- Lindsey Graham, close ally of the administration, really putting this at the vice president's feet at this point. And so, it's not just the comments that he's making publicly, the vice president. It's these other comments coming from allies of the Trump administration putting this, if it doesn't work out, at the vice president's feet.
paragraphs.cn: Well, that really -- but it's a very interesting comment because he really was -- Lindsey Graham, a close ally of the administration, really blamed the Vice President for this. So, it's not just the Vice President's public statements, but also these comments from Trump administration allies, shifting blame to the Vice President if things go wrong.

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.en: Last week, I actually talked to the vice president about the Iraq War, and he told me that he doesn't think it's going to become a quagmire, that we're not even going to be talking about this a year from now. So, the one that -- the statement that you laid out isn't the only mile marker that he's putting forward here.
paragraphs.cn: Last week, I actually spoke with the Vice President about the Iraq war, and he told me he doesn't think it will become a quagmire, and we won't even be talking about it a year from now. So, that statement you mentioned isn't the only milestone he's set.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. But can I translate your statement? Lindsey Graham, the hawk, who's been cheering this on the whole time, is trying to put the actual deal at the feet of J.D. Vance, who's not been cheering this on the whole time, so that if it goes sideways, everyone can say somehow Vance was behind a bad deal?
paragraphs.cn: Yes. But can I translate what you're saying? Lindsey Graham, the hawk who has been cheering this on, is trying to pin the responsibility for the actual deal on J.D. Vance, who hasn't been cheering it on, so if things go wrong, everyone can say Vance somehow supported a bad deal?

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.en: Well, I'm saying that Lindsey Graham is -- in calling him the architect of this negotiation, this deal that's coming forward -- and the vice president was very, very involved -- is putting the onus on him. If this doesn't work out. I'm not --
paragraphs.cn: Well, I mean Lindsey Graham -- in calling him the architect of these negotiations, the upcoming deal -- and the Vice President is very, very deeply involved -- is shifting blame to him. If things don't go well. I'm not --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, yes. So, if it goes bad, everyone can be like, It was Vance.
paragraphs.cn: So, yes. So, if things go bad, everyone can say it's Vance's fault.

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.en: And he's saying that time will tell. I'm saying that politically, it becomes a risk for the vice president --
paragraphs.cn: And he says time will tell. I mean, from a political standpoint, this becomes a risk for the Vice President --

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Chambers
paragraphs.en: -- at that point, when you have allies of the administration saying he was the architect.
paragraphs.cn: -- at that point, when an administration ally calls him the architect.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Interesting. OK. We're going to talk more about this as we learn more about this memorandum this week. And then after the break on CNN THIS MORNING, did you know that the U.K. is the latest country to ban kids under the age of 16 from all social media? We'll find out whether these bans actually work. Plus --
paragraphs.cn: Interesting. Okay. We'll discuss this further this week as we learn more about the contents of this memo. And after a break on CNN This Morning, you know, the UK is the latest country to ban all social media for children under 16? We'll look at whether these bans actually work. Also --

paragraphs.speaker: Dana White
paragraphs.en: I can't afford it. There's no (EXPLETIVE DELETED) way we can do this again.
paragraphs.cn: I can't afford it. We absolutely cannot do this again.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The head of the UFC, Dana White, says he won't be doing the White House fight night again, despite it being what he called an overwhelming success. In the meantime, good morning, very early morning to Seattle, which is hosting a World Cup match today. Belgium and Egypt face off at 3 p.m. Eastern.
paragraphs.cn: UFC President Dana White says that although he thinks the White House Fight Night was a huge success, he won't do it again. Meanwhile, good morning, very early morning greetings to Seattle, where a World Cup match will be held today. Belgium and Egypt will face off at 3 p.m. Eastern Time.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: For the people who said there shouldn't be a cage fight at the White House, what do you say?
paragraphs.cn: What do you think of those who say the White House shouldn't host a cage fight?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: They might be right, but since it's here, we're going to be here.
paragraphs.cn: They might be right, but since we're here, we'll stay.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I don't agree with everything Donald Trump has -- has been doing this term, but I'm here, and I'm enjoying the moment.
paragraphs.cn: I don't agree with everything Donald Trump has done this term, but I'm here, enjoying the moment.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, UFC fight on the White House lawn. Whether you agreed with it or not, it happened. It was real. Weather slightly delayed the start as President Trump took his seat ringside alongside UFC CEO Dana White. Officially, the event was held on Flag Day, which just also happened to be Donald Trump's 80th birthday. And at one point, the crowd sang "Happy Birthday." And after several fights, the night wrapped up just after 1 a.m. With President Trump greeting the new champion in the Octagon.
paragraphs.cn: Well, UFC fighting on the White House lawn. Whether you agree or not, it happened. It's real. The weather slightly delayed the start time, and President Trump sat ringside with UFC CEO Dana White. Officially, the event was held on Flag Day, which also happened to be Donald Trump's 80th birthday. At one point, the crowd sang Happy Birthday. After several fights, the event ended after 1 a.m., and President Trump greeted the new champion in the octagon.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The event was the first of its kind on the White House grounds. So, we're going to ask, how is it going to be remembered, both for the sport and in U.S. history? Joining me now is Mike Leon. He's the host of the podcast, "Can We Please Talk?" Good morning, Mike. How are you? MIKE LEON, HOST, "CAN WE PLEASE TALK?": Good to see you, Audie. How's everything?
paragraphs.cn: This is the first event of its kind on White House grounds. So, we ask, how will it be remembered, both for the sport and in American history? Joining me now is Mike Leon. He is the host of the podcast Can We Talk? Good morning, Mike. How are you? Mike Leon, host of Can We Talk?: Great to see you, Audie. Everything good?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, as a fight, was it good? Like, is the card, the tournament? I know the Polymarket, Kalshi prediction markets did very well with all of these different bouts. But what was it like just as a fan?
paragraphs.cn: So, as a fight, was it exciting? Like, the fight card, the tournament? I know Polymarket, Kalshi prediction markets did very well on these different fights. But just as a fan, how did it feel?

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Yes, just as a fan, you know, working in the combat sports space and overseeing productions. From a production standpoint, it really was immersive, expansive. I mean, we could talk about you couldn't watch it unless you had a Paramount+ subscription. It wasn't free over the air like on CBS, which I thought the president probably would have done, or at least Dana White would have done.
paragraphs.cn: Yes, just as a fan, you know, I work in the combat sports space and produce. From a production standpoint, it was immersive, grand scale. I mean, we can say, unless you have a Paramount+ subscription, you couldn't see it. It wasn't free-to-air like CBS, which I thought the President might do, or at least Dana White would.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: But you've got to recoup those costs of the 60 million that they spent to -- to do all of this and the Claw and everything that they implemented. But just from a TV perspective, watching it, the show, the programing that they did. Chris Weidman is a friend of mine who happened to do some of the pre-fight coverage there. It really was a true event outside of a typical arena or some type of setting that houses combat sports. But there's a bunch of things underlying, in terms of the event with respect to politics.
paragraphs.cn: But you have to recoup the $60 million cost they spent, and everything they implemented like the claws. But just from a TV perspective, watching it, the show and production they did. Chris Weidman is a friend of mine, and he happened to be there doing some pre-fight coverage. It was truly an event beyond a typical arena or combat sports venue. But politically, there are many underlying factors behind this event.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: But from a fight night perspective, Audie, it actually was a pretty decent sporting event.
paragraphs.cn: But from a fight night perspective, Audie, it was actually a pretty good sporting event.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And to your point, Dana White pretty much was, like, I'm not going to do this again just because of the sheer expense.
paragraphs.cn: Seperti yang Anda katakan, Dana White pada dasarnya mengatakan bahwa dia tidak akan melakukannya lagi hanya karena biaya yang sangat besar.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But I want to get to something which is who was, you know, in the Octagon and who was not. You had UFC middleweight champion Sean Strickland. He was off the card. And he's the reigning champ, right, middleweight champ. And Strickland thinks he knows why. He basically has turned down -- he's been this outspoken, you know, critic of Trump on social media.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi saya ingin berbicara tentang siapa yang ada di dalam oktagon dan siapa yang tidak. Juara kelas menengah UFC Sean Strickland tidak ada dalam daftar pertarungan. Dia adalah juara saat ini, kan, juara kelas menengah. Strickland mengira dia tahu alasannya. Dia pada dasarnya menolak -- -- dia secara blak-blakan mengkritik Trump di media sosial.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He says that he stopped supporting Trump after, quote, "Israel made him bomb Iran the first time." Another thing he's posted, saying that he got a call from UFC saying he, quote, "wasn't cleared" by the White House. And honestly, he showed up anyway; in an extreme MMA on-brand situation was escorted out by park police. I mean, it actually became like a bit of a thing here. Help me make sense of this. It's a big UFC night. A big UFC star's getting hauled out with all this security. What's the politics?
paragraphs.cn: Dia mengatakan dia tidak lagi mendukung Trump karena "Israel membuatnya mengebom Iran untuk pertama kalinya". Dia juga memposting bahwa dia mendapat telepon dari UFC yang mengatakan bahwa Gedung Putih menganggapnya "tidak disetujui". Sejujurnya, dia tetap muncul; dalam situasi gaya MMA yang ekstrem, dia dikawal keluar oleh polisi taman. Maksud saya, ini benar-benar menjadi masalah. Bantu saya mengerti. Ini adalah malam UFC yang penting. Seorang bintang besar UFC diseret oleh begitu banyak petugas keamanan. Apa faktor politiknya?

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: I mean, remember, there's an element of how UFC is also in partnership with the WWE, right? There's a performative element with some of that, for Strickland doing that. And there's also guys who fought on the card. I don't know if you saw the video, Audie, of the -- the person who said the comments about Michelle Obama in the post-fight interview with Joe Rogan, which was disgusting. There are some things that are, from a performance standpoint, always going to mirror what we kind of see in WWE.
paragraphs.cn: Maksud saya, ingat, UFC juga bekerja sama dengan WWE, kan? Ada elemen pertunjukan dalam apa yang dilakukan Strickland. Ada juga beberapa orang yang berpartisipasi dalam pertarungan. Saya tidak tahu apakah Anda melihat videonya, Audie, orang yang mengatakan hal menjijikkan tentang Michelle Obama kepada Joe Rogan dalam wawancara pasca-pertarungan. Dari sudut pandang pertunjukan, selalu ada hal-hal yang mencerminkan apa yang kita lihat di WWE.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Strickland is right, to a certain extent. There's a reason why he was left off the card. Non-politics perspective wise. There were a few other fights that actually dropped out from this card. But in terms of the event overall, like I said, it was a pretty solid event. But to make sense of it, Audie, I mean, they get punched in the head; they grapple. Sometimes people say different things, and they have different political leanings. This is what you get in the combat sports space.
paragraphs.cn: Strickland benar sampai batas tertentu. Ada alasan dia tidak ada dalam daftar pertarungan. Dari sudut pandang non-politik, ada beberapa pertarungan lain yang sebenarnya mundur. Tapi untuk keseluruhan acara, seperti yang saya katakan, ini adalah acara yang cukup solid. Tapi untuk memahaminya, Audie, maksud saya, mereka dipukul di kepala; mereka bergulat. Terkadang orang mengatakan hal yang berbeda, memiliki kecenderungan politik yang berbeda. Itulah yang Anda dapatkan dalam olahraga tarung.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: I remember working an event where one of our fighters, before the election in '24, wore Trump trunks. And I remember saying to him in the locker room, "Could you not?" Like, again, freedom of speech. You can wear whatever you want, but like, you're going to get more attention doing this. And he's, like, I'm going to do it anyway. And then he went out there and did it anyway. So, that's -- if you want me to make sense of it, Audie, that's the way to make sense of it. There is no sense.
paragraphs.cn: Saya ingat dalam sebuah acara, salah satu petarung kami mengenakan celana pendek Trump sebelum pemilu 2024. Saya ingat mengatakan kepadanya di ruang ganti: "Bisakah kamu tidak melakukannya?" Lagi pula, kebebasan berbicara. Anda bisa memakai apa pun yang Anda mau, tetapi Anda akan mendapatkan lebih banyak perhatian karenanya. Dia berkata, saya akan tetap memakainya. Lalu dia keluar dan tetap memakainya. Jadi, jika Anda ingin saya menjelaskannya, Audie, inilah penjelasannya. Tidak ada logika.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: They get punched in the head for a living.
paragraphs.cn: Mereka mencari nafkah dengan dipukul di kepala.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Does this win over some of the young men that Trump is losing in some of his approval ratings?
paragraphs.cn: Apakah ini bisa memenangkan beberapa pria muda yang hilang dari Trump dalam jajak pendapat?

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Audie, you and I talked about this last week when I was in studio with you. Trump has always used the combat sports space as this outlet and vehicle to talk to voters, whether it be his stuff that he did in the boxing space years ago, his relationship with Dana White, obviously with the Ellison family, with Paramount. He has always been in the combat sports space, because he thinks it makes him seem relatable, that he loves these sports. And these are the type of fans.
paragraphs.cn: Audie, minggu lalu saya membahas ini dengan Anda di studio. Trump telah menggunakan olahraga tarung sebagai saluran dan alat untuk berkomunikasi dengan pemilih, baik itu apa yang dia lakukan di dunia tinju bertahun-tahun lalu, hubungannya dengan Dana White, dan jelas hubungannya dengan keluarga Ellison dan Paramount. Dia aktif di olahraga tarung karena dia pikir itu membuatnya tampak mudah didekati, dia mencintai olahraga ini. Dan inilah para penggemar semacam itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: And again, speaking with conservative voters over years in different cities, they've talked about his attendance at these events. And Democrats don't go to these type of things, whereas Trump does. I told you last week, Ruben Gallego and I've had this conversation about Democrats potentially leaning into, like, boxing events, to the relationships that I have in the combat sports space. And it's just -- there's voters there that I feel are being left out. And they feel they're being left out of the conversation.
paragraphs.cn: Sekali lagi, selama bertahun-tahun berbicara dengan pemilih konservatif di berbagai kota, mereka menyebutkan kehadirannya di acara-acara ini. Demokrat tidak menghadiri acara semacam ini, sementara Trump akan pergi. Minggu lalu saya memberi tahu Anda, Ruben Gallego dan saya mendiskusikan kemungkinan Demokrat cenderung berpartisipasi dalam acara tinju, dan koneksi saya di dunia olahraga tarung. Hanya saja -- -- ada beberapa pemilih di sana yang saya rasa diabaikan. Dan mereka sendiri merasa dikecualikan dari percakapan.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: var CNN = CNN || {};

vocabulary.level: Latar Belakang Nama Khusus · Tingkat Lanjut
vocabulary.cn: Selat Hormuz (jalur air strategis yang menghubungkan Teluk Persia dengan Samudra Hindia)
vocabulary.en: a narrow strait between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman, crucial for global oil shipments
vocabulary.excerpt: both the U.S. and Iran say there's an agreement to open the Strait of Hormuz on Friday.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Amerika Serikat dan Iran sama-sama mengatakan telah mencapai kesepakatan untuk membuka Selat Hormuz pada hari Jumat.

vocabulary.cn: Negosiator; perunding
vocabulary.en: a person who conducts negotiations
vocabulary.excerpt: The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Orang Iran adalah negosiator yang sangat sulit.

vocabulary.cn: 酸葡萄心理（因得不到而贬低）
vocabulary.en: an expression meaning to disparage something one cannot have
vocabulary.excerpt: True or just sour grapes?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这是真的还是酸葡萄心理？

vocabulary.cn: 跳伞者；跳伞运动员
vocabulary.en: a person who performs skydiving
vocabulary.excerpt: A plane full of skydivers crashes right after takeoff.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 一架满载跳伞者的飞机起飞后立即坠毁。

vocabulary.cn: 住院的；被送入医院的
vocabulary.en: admitted to a hospital for treatment
vocabulary.excerpt: Senator Mitch McConnell hospitalized.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 参议员米奇·麦康奈尔住院。

vocabulary.cn: 社交媒体禁令
vocabulary.en: a prohibition on the use of social media platforms
vocabulary.excerpt: But will a new social media ban actually work?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 但新的社交媒体禁令真的会奏效吗？

vocabulary.cn: 终极格斗冠军赛（Ultimate Fighting Championship）
vocabulary.en: a mixed martial arts promotion company
vocabulary.excerpt: UFC fight night at the White House lawn.
vocabulary.exampleCn: UFC格斗之夜在白宫草坪举行。

vocabulary.cn: 斯坦利杯（NHL冠军奖杯）
vocabulary.en: the championship trophy awarded annually to the National Hockey League (NHL) playoff winner
vocabulary.excerpt: the Carolina Hurricanes are Stanley Cup champions.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 卡罗来纳飓风队是斯坦利杯冠军。

vocabulary.cn: 完封（体育比赛中一方未得分）
vocabulary.en: a game in which the opposing team fails to score
vocabulary.excerpt: capping off the season with a shutout over the Las Vegas Golden Knights.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 以完封拉斯维加斯金骑士队结束赛季。

vocabulary.cn: 外交突破
vocabulary.en: a significant advance in diplomatic relations
vocabulary.excerpt: A diplomatic breakthrough: The U.S. and Iran say they have reached a deal.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 外交突破：美国和伊朗称已达成协议。

vocabulary.cn: 停火；停战
vocabulary.en: a temporary suspension of fighting
vocabulary.excerpt: Sometimes these ceasefires take a little bit of time to take root.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 有时这些停火需要一点时间才能扎根。

vocabulary.cn: （讽刺）和谐一致；唱赞歌（源自灵歌，常指不切实际的乐观）
vocabulary.en: used ironically to refer to a naive or overly optimistic view of harmony
vocabulary.excerpt: I'm not going to say that everybody is going to sing kumbaya tomorrow.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我不会说明天大家就会唱起和谐赞歌。

vocabulary.cn: 谅解备忘录（MOU，非正式协议）
vocabulary.en: a non-binding agreement outlining the terms of a deal
vocabulary.excerpt: This memorandum of understanding was reached, despite our complete distrust of our enemies.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 尽管我们完全不相信敌人，但这份谅解备忘录还是达成了。

vocabulary.cn: 实施；执行
vocabulary.en: the process of putting a decision or plan into effect
vocabulary.excerpt: We have specific plans in place to monitor the implementation of U.S. commitments.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们有具体计划来监督美国承诺的执行。

vocabulary.cn: 使偏离轨道；破坏（计划、进程）
vocabulary.en: to obstruct or prevent (a process) from continuing
vocabulary.excerpt: So, could this derail things?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 那么，这会不会破坏事情？

vocabulary.cn: 强硬的；不妥协的
vocabulary.en: uncompromising; strict
vocabulary.excerpt: this regime, in particular, is more hardline than the previous regime.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这个政权尤其比之前的政权更加强硬。

vocabulary.cn: 棘手的；麻烦的
vocabulary.en: causing difficulty; problematic
vocabulary.excerpt: It does not address the thorniest issues.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 它没有解决最棘手的问题。

vocabulary.cn: 高浓缩铀（可用于核武器）
vocabulary.en: uranium with a high concentration of the isotope U-235, suitable for nuclear weapons
vocabulary.excerpt: the highly enriched uranium that is still sitting in Iran.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 仍存在于伊朗的高浓缩铀。

vocabulary.cn: 军事行动（委婉语，指动武）
vocabulary.en: military action involving the use of force
vocabulary.excerpt: the United States is not going to want to start up kinetic action right before the midterms.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 美国不希望在中期选举前发起军事行动。

vocabulary.cn: 中期选举（美国国会选举）
vocabulary.en: elections held in the middle of a presidential term
vocabulary.excerpt: right before the midterms.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 就在中期选举之前。

vocabulary.cn: 交叉火力；交火
vocabulary.en: gunfire from two or more directions
vocabulary.excerpt: if a drone is launched from Iran or there's crossfire between the two sides.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 如果伊朗发射无人机或双方发生交火。

vocabulary.cn: 代理人；代理（如代理人战争）
vocabulary.en: a person or entity authorized to act on behalf of another
vocabulary.excerpt: proxies like Hezbollah.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Seperti kelompok proksi seperti Hizbullah.

vocabulary.cn: rudal balistik
vocabulary.en: a missile that follows a ballistic trajectory
vocabulary.excerpt: It doesn't even talk about ballistic missiles.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Bahkan tidak menyebutkan rudal balistik.

vocabulary.level: Latar belakang nama khusus · Tingkat lanjut
vocabulary.cn: Rencana Aksi Komprehensif Bersama (Kesepakatan Nuklir Iran)
vocabulary.en: Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action, the 2015 nuclear deal with Iran
vocabulary.excerpt: you might not have agreed with the JCPOA.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Anda mungkin tidak setuju dengan Rencana Aksi Komprehensif Bersama.

sentences.en: Today in the group chat, both the U.S. and Iran say there's an agreement to open the Strait of Hormuz on Friday.
sentences.cn: Hari ini dalam diskusi grup, baik AS maupun Iran mengatakan telah mencapai kesepakatan untuk membuka Selat Hormuz pada hari Jumat.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama (both the U.S. and Iran say) + klausa objek (there's an agreement to open the Strait of Hormuz on Friday), diawali dengan keterangan waktu.
sentences.analysis: Ekspresi ringkas berita: 'group chat' merujuk pada sesi diskusi dalam acara; 'there's an agreement to open' adalah struktur there be, infinitif menyatakan tujuan.

sentences.en: A plane full of skydivers crashes right after takeoff, killing everyone on board.
sentences.cn: Sebuah pesawat penuh penerjun payung jatuh segera setelah lepas landas, menewaskan semua orang di dalamnya.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama (A plane full of skydivers crashes right after takeoff) + keterangan penyerta (killing everyone on board), frasa partisip present menyatakan hasil.
sentences.analysis: Frasa partisip sebagai keterangan hasil: 'killing everyone on board' menjelaskan akibat kecelakaan; 'full of skydivers' adalah frasa adjektiva sebagai atribut di belakang.

sentences.en: In the U.K., they want to give their kids their childhood back.
sentences.cn: Di Inggris, mereka ingin mengembalikan masa kecil kepada anak-anak mereka.
sentences.structure: Keterangan tempat (In the U.K.) + klausa utama (they want to give their kids their childhood back), struktur objek ganda.
sentences.analysis: Objek ganda: 'give their kids their childhood back' di mana 'their kids' adalah objek tidak langsung, 'their childhood back' adalah objek langsung; 'back' adalah adverbia yang berarti mengembalikan.

sentences.en: A diplomatic breakthrough: The U.S. and Iran say they have reached a deal to end the war and to reopen the strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: Terobosan diplomatik: AS dan Iran mengatakan telah mencapai kesepakatan untuk mengakhiri perang dan membuka kembali Selat Hormuz.
sentences.structure: Struktur aposisi (A diplomatic breakthrough) + klausa utama (The U.S. and Iran say) + klausa objek (they have reached a deal to end the war and to reopen the strait of Hormuz), infinitif paralel sebagai atribut.
sentences.analysis: Infinitif paralel sebagai atribut di belakang: 'to end the war and to reopen the strait of Hormuz' menerangkan 'deal'; titik dua memperkenalkan konten spesifik.

sentences.en: Sometimes these ceasefires take a little bit of time to take root.
sentences.cn: Terkadang perjanjian gencatan senjata ini membutuhkan waktu untuk berakar.
sentences.structure: 主句（these ceasefires take a little bit of time）+ 不定式状语（to take root）。
sentences.analysis: 固定搭配：'take time to do sth.' 意为"花时间做某事"；'take root' 比喻"扎根、生效"。

sentences.en: I'm not going to say that everybody is going to sing kumbaya tomorrow.
sentences.cn: 我不会说明天大家就会唱起《Kumbaya》。
sentences.structure: 主句（I'm not going to say）+ 宾语从句（that everybody is going to sing kumbaya tomorrow）。
sentences.analysis: 文化引用：'sing kumbaya' 源自民谣，常用来讽刺不切实际的和谐或团结；'be going to' 表将来。

sentences.en: But I do think we took a major, major step.
sentences.cn: 但我确实认为我们迈出了非常重大的一步。
sentences.structure: 主句（I do think）+ 宾语从句（we took a major, major step），'do' 为强调助动词。
sentences.analysis: 强调结构：'do' 用于强调谓语动词 'think'；'major, major' 重复表程度加深。

sentences.en: Tehran saying that 60 days of nuclear negotiations will begin once the U.S. meets three commitments.
sentences.cn: 德黑兰表示，一旦美国满足三项承诺，为期60天的核谈判将开始。
sentences.structure: 独立主格结构（Tehran saying）+ 宾语从句（that 60 days of nuclear negotiations will begin once the U.S. meets three commitments），'once' 引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 独立主格：'Tehran saying' 为现在分词独立结构，相当于 'Tehran says'；'once' 引导条件状语从句，意为"一旦"。

sentences.en: This memorandum of understanding was reached, despite our complete distrust of our enemies.
sentences.cn: 这份谅解备忘录已经达成，尽管我们完全不相信我们的敌人。
sentences.structure: 主句（This memorandum of understanding was reached）+ 让步状语（despite our complete distrust of our enemies）。
sentences.analysis: 介词短语表让步：'despite' 后接名词短语；'distrust of' 为固定搭配，意为"对……的不信任"。

sentences.en: Our trust lies in our military strength, our diplomatic capabilities, and the support and unity of our own people.
sentences.cn: 我们的信任在于我们的军事实力、外交能力以及本国人民的支持与团结。
sentences.structure: 主句（Our trust lies in）+ 并列宾语（our military strength, our diplomatic capabilities, and the support and unity of our own people）。
sentences.analysis: 并列结构：三个名词短语并列作 'lies in' 的宾语；'lie in' 意为"在于"。

sentences.en: That is why the implementation of this memorandum of understanding is extremely important.
sentences.cn: 这就是为什么这份谅解备忘录的执行极其重要。
sentences.structure: 主句（That is）+ 表语从句（why the implementation of this memorandum of understanding is extremely important）。
sentences.analysis: 表语从句：'why' 引导的从句作 'is' 的表语，说明原因；'implementation of' 为名词短语。

sentences.en: But a U.S. official is saying that no funds will be released before Iran implements its commitments.
sentences.cn: 但一位美国官员表示，在伊朗履行其承诺之前，不会释放任何资金。
sentences.structure: 主句（a U.S. official is saying）+ 宾语从句（that no funds will be released before Iran implements its commitments），'before' 引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 时间状语从句：'before' 引导的从句表示"在……之前"；'release funds' 意为"释放资金"。

sentences.en: The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators, coupled with the fact that they're having a very hard time getting guidance from their supreme leader.
sentences.cn: 伊朗人是极其难缠的谈判者，再加上他们很难从最高领袖那里获得指导。
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators) + keterangan penyerta (coupled with the fact that...), 'that' memimpin klausa apositif.
sentences.analysis: Frasa partisip lampau sebagai keterangan penyerta: 'coupled with' berarti "ditambah dengan"; klausa apositif 'that they're having a very hard time getting guidance' menjelaskan 'the fact'.

sentences.en: And they're not always on the same page within their team, between the civilians and military.
sentences.cn: Dan mereka tidak selalu sepaham dalam tim mereka, antara sipil dan militer.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (they're not always on the same page) + keterangan tempat (within their team, between the civilians and military).
sentences.analysis: Frasa tetap: 'on the same page' berarti "sepaham"; 'between...and...' menunjukkan rentang.

sentences.en: I think what's interesting about this memorandum of understanding, or this new agreement, is it's only there to open the straits.
sentences.cn: Saya pikir yang menarik dari nota kesepahaman ini, atau perjanjian baru ini, adalah bahwa itu hanya ada untuk membuka selat.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (I think) + klausa objek (what's interesting about this memorandum of understanding, or this new agreement, is it's only there to open the straits), di mana 'what' memimpin klausa subjek, 'is' diikuti klausa predikat.
sentences.analysis: Klausa subjek: 'what's interesting...' sebagai subjek; 'or this new agreement' sebagai sisipan, menjelaskan 'memorandum of understanding'; 'only there to' menyatakan tujuan.

sentences.en: It does not address the thorniest issues, which we know Iran is really going to drag its feet on, which are the nuclear -- its nuclear program and the highly enriched uranium that is still sitting in Iran.
sentences.cn: Itu tidak membahas isu-isu paling rumit, yang kita tahu Iran akan sengaja mengulur-ulur waktu, yaitu program nuklirnya dan uranium yang sangat diperkaya yang masih disimpan di Iran.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (It does not address the thorniest issues) + klausa relatif non-restriktif (which we know Iran is really going to drag its feet on) + klausa relatif non-restriktif lain (which are the nuclear...), di mana 'that' memimpin klausa relatif yang menerangkan 'uranium'.
sentences.analysis: Klausa relatif bersarang: 'which' pertama merujuk pada 'issues', 'which' kedua merujuk pada 'thorniest issues'; 'drag one's feet on' berarti "sengaja mengulur-ulur waktu"; 'highly enriched uranium' adalah istilah teknis.

sentences.en: And Iran, like -- like Mike Waltz was saying, Iran knows how to play the game to draw the United States out.
sentences.cn: Dan Iran, seperti yang dikatakan Mike Waltz, Iran tahu cara memainkan permainan ini untuk memancing Amerika.
sentences.structure: Sisipan (like Mike Waltz was saying) + kalimat utama (Iran knows how to play the game to draw the United States out), 'how to' adalah struktur kata tanya + infinitif.
sentences.analysis: Sisipan: 'like Mike Waltz was saying' menunjukkan kutipan; 'play the game' metafora "bermain strategi"; 'draw...out' berarti "memancing, mengeluarkan".

sentences.en: If we're talking about negotiating, at minimum, within 60 days, that is bringing us up right up to the midterms.
sentences.cn: Jika kita berbicara tentang negosiasi, setidaknya dalam 60 hari, itu akan membawa kita langsung ke pemilihan paruh waktu.
sentences.structure: Klausa kondisi (If we're talking about negotiating) + sisipan (at minimum) + kalimat utama (that is bringing us up right up to the midterms), 'within 60 days' sebagai keterangan waktu.
sentences.analysis: Kalimat kondisi: 'if' memimpin kondisi nyata; 'at minimum' sebagai sisipan, berarti "setidaknya"; 'bring up to' berarti "membawa ke"; 'midterms' merujuk pada pemilihan paruh waktu.

sentences.en: And I think Iran knows that the United States is not going to want to start up kinetic action right before the midterms, right before voting starts.
sentences.cn: Dan saya pikir Iran tahu bahwa Amerika tidak akan ingin melakukan aksi militer tepat sebelum pemilihan paruh waktu, sebelum pemungutan suara dimulai.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (I think) + klausa objek (Iran knows that...), 'that' memimpin klausa objek, di mana 'right before' memimpin keterangan waktu.
sentences.analysis: Klausa objek bertingkat: 'Iran knows' diikuti klausa 'that'; 'kinetic action' adalah istilah militer, berarti "aksi militer"; 'right before' menekankan "tepat sebelum".

sentences.en: I think both sides could say today that the strait is open and that they're clearing the mines from the strait.
sentences.cn: Saya pikir kedua belah pihak hari ini dapat mengatakan bahwa selat itu terbuka, dan bahwa mereka sedang membersihkan ranjau dari selat tersebut.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (I think) + klausa objek (both sides could say today that...and that...), dua 'that' memimpin klausa objek yang setara.
sentences.analysis: Klausa objek setara: dua klausa 'that' setara sebagai objek dari 'say'; 'clear the mines' berarti "membersihkan ranjau".

topics.title: Kesepakatan AS-Iran tentang Selat Hormuz
topics.content: Amerika Serikat dan Iran mencapai nota kesepahaman, berencana membuka Selat Hormuz pada Jumat, 19 Juni 2026. Iran mengajukan tiga prasyarat: mencabut blokade angkatan laut, menghentikan operasi militer, dan mencairkan dana miliaran dolar. Pejabat AS membantah segera memberikan dana, mengatakan Iran harus memenuhi komitmen terlebih dahulu. Kesepakatan tidak mencakup masalah nuklir (nuclear program) dan rudal balistik (ballistic missiles), dianggap sebagai pengaturan sementara.
topics.keywords: Strait of Hormuz · memorandum of understanding · nuclear program · ballistic missiles

topics.title: Ketidakpuasan Israel terhadap Kesepakatan
topics.content: Israel menyatakan ketidakpuasan terhadap kesepakatan AS-Iran, karena dianggap tidak menyelesaikan masalah proksi (proxies) yang didukung Iran seperti Hizbullah (Hezbollah) dan rudal balistik. Israel tidak terlibat dalam negosiasi dan mungkin melanjutkan operasi militer di Lebanon. Presiden AS Donald Trump secara terbuka mengkritik Perdana Menteri Israel Benjamin Netanyahu (Bibi), khawatir tindakan Israel akan merusak kesepakatan.
topics.keywords: proxies · Hezbollah · Benjamin Netanyahu · military operations

topics.title: Pemilu Paruh Waktu AS dan Strategi Negosiasi Iran
topics.content: Analis menunjukkan bahwa Iran menggunakan jadwal negosiasi untuk mendorong titik-titik kunci menjelang pemilu paruh waktu (midterms) AS, sehingga AS enggan mengambil tindakan militer (kinetic action) sebelum pemilu. Gaya negosiasi Iran keras (hardline), terdapat perbedaan pendapat antara sipil dan militer, tetapi pandai menunda dan menekan.

topics.title: Berita Lainnya
topics.content: Acara ini juga melaporkan: sebuah pesawat yang membawa penerjun payung (skydivers) jatuh setelah lepas landas, semua penumpang tewas; Senator Mitch McConnell dirawat di rumah sakit; Inggris berencana memberlakukan larangan media sosial (social media ban) untuk melindungi remaja; UFC (Ultimate Fighting Championship) mengadakan pertandingan di halaman Gedung Putih; Carolina Hurricanes menutup (shutout) Vegas Golden Knights dan memenangkan Piala Stanley (Stanley Cup).
topics.keywords: skydivers · Mitch McConnell · social media ban · UFC · Stanley Cup · shutout

quiz.question: Dalam kalimat asli 'The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators.', apa arti 'negotiators'?
options: A. Perunding
B. Mediator
C. Pengamat
D. Penerjemah
quiz.explanation: Negotiator berarti 'perunding', yaitu orang yang berpartisipasi dalam negosiasi. Dalam kalimat asli, duta besar AS menggambarkan Iran sebagai 'difficult negotiators' (perunding yang sulit).

quiz.question: Dalam kalimat asli 'It does not address the thorniest issues.', apa arti 'thorniest'?
options: A. Paling tajam
B. Paling sulit
C. Paling berbahaya
D. Paling kompleks
quiz.explanation: Thorny secara harfiah berarti 'berduri', secara kiasan berarti 'sulit, rumit'. Thorniest adalah bentuk superlatif, berarti 'paling sulit'.

quiz.question: Dalam kalimat asli 'the United States is not going to want to start up kinetic action right before the midterms.', apa yang dimaksud dengan 'kinetic action'?
options: A. 外交行动
B. 经济制裁
C. 军事行动
D. 法律行动
quiz.explanation: kinetic action 是军事委婉语，意为'军事行动'，指动用武力。

quiz.question: 根据报道，美伊协议的主要目的是什么？
options: A. 彻底解决伊朗核问题
B. 开放霍尔木兹海峡
C. 解除对伊朗的所有制裁
D. 建立长期和平关系
quiz.explanation: 报道明确指出协议是为了 'open the Strait of Hormuz on Friday'，即开放霍尔木兹海峡。核问题等更棘手议题未被涵盖。

quiz.question: 伊朗提出的三个前提条件不包括以下哪一项？
options: A. 美国结束海军封锁
B. 美国停止军事行动
C. 美国释放冻结的伊朗资金
D. 美国承认伊朗核权利
quiz.explanation: 报道中伊朗要求：结束海军封锁、停止军事行动、释放数十亿美元冻结资金。未提及承认核权利。

quiz.question: 为什么分析人士认为伊朗将谈判拖延至60天后？
options: A. 等待国际社会介入
B. 利用美国中期选举前的政治敏感期
C. 等待新领导人上任
D. 为军事准备争取时间
quiz.explanation: 报道指出，60天谈判期将 'bringing us up right up to the midterms'，伊朗知道美国不愿在选举前发动军事行动，从而获得谈判优势。
